mappings.rationaleAI: Core text covering individual activities (food getting, aggression, reproduction), kin selection, alarm calls, parental care, and social behaviour (A, C).
Directly covers habituation, sensitization, classical and operant conditioning, and observational learning in animals (B.1, B.2, B.4).
Directly relevant to classical conditioning, a foundational form of associative learning central to this section (B.2).
Examines problem solving, reasoning, and insight as cognitive capacities observable in higher animals (B.5).
Explores Darwin's theory of mate choice, sexual dimorphism, and reproductive behaviour including intrasexual competition and intersexual selection (A.6).
Illustrates mammalian behavioural capacities from basic locomotion and foraging to complex social structures and brain-driven learning (A, B).
Examines insect social behaviour, colony dynamics, and pheromone-based communication in eusocial species such as ants, bees, and termites (C.1, C.2, C.4).
Reveals the neural substrates of learning, spatial cognition, and memory that underlie higher behavioural capacities (B.3, B.5).
Investigates memory formation and spatial memory, directly relevant to maze learning, place cells, and animal navigation (B.3).
Analyzes perceptual processing including motion perception and pattern recognition relevant to perceptual and observational learning (B.4).
Explores cognitive abilities, working memory, and problem-solving capacities relevant to complex reasoning in higher animals (B.5).
Addresses foraging behaviour, optimal diet theory, and food-getting strategies directly relevant to basic food-getting activities (A.1).
Introduces principles of navigation -- landmarks, dead reckoning, celestial cues -- relevant to spatial learning and animal navigation abilities (B.3).
Addresses aggression, group dynamics, obedience, and social interaction relevant to the dynamics of social behaviour (A.4, C.3, C.4).
